questions.question_text: A person walks 40 km at a speed of 5 km/hr, then runs 30 km at a speed of 10 km/hr. What is the ratio of walking time to running time?

Suppose the ratio of income to expenditure is 8 : 5. If income rises by 40% and expenditure falls by 10%, and the initial expenditure is $$ \text{₹}10,000 $$, what is the amount (in $$ \text{₹} $$) of saving after these changes?

The area of a regular polygon with a side of 11 cm is $$ 247.5 \, \text{cm}^2 $$. If the perpendicular distance from the centre to the side of the polygon is 9 cm, what is the number of sides of the polygon?
The largest four-digit number which when divided by 6, 10 and 14 leaves remainder 3 in each case is:
If $$ 5^{x-6} \times 6^{x-1} = 180 $$, then the value of $$ \frac{2x-1}{2x+3} $$ is:
A's expenditure is double the difference between his savings and expenditure. What percentage of his salary is his expenditure?

A customer is given two options for purchasing a product priced at $$ \text{₹}8,000 $$:Option 1: A single discount of 30%.Option 2: Successive discounts of 20% and 15%.Which option provides a greater discount, and by how much?

Pipe A can fill a cistern in 10 hours, pipe B in 15 hours, and pipe C can empty it in 30 hours. All three pipes are opened together. After 6 hours, pipe B is closed, but B and C continue working. How much total time will it take to completely fill the cistern?

ABCD is a quadrilateral in which $$ AB \parallel DC $$, and E and F are the mid points of the diagonals AC and BD, respectively. If AB = 25 cm, DC = 44 cm, BC = 27 cm, and AD = 17 cm, then what is the height (in cm) of EF?
If the median of the data $$ \frac{x}{5}, \frac{x}{3}, \frac{x}{2}, \frac{2x}{5}, \frac{3x}{4} $$ and $$ \frac{3x}{4} $$, where $$ x > 0 $$ is 8.4, then find the value of x.

An article is sold at a profit of 32%. If the cost price is increased by $$ \text{₹}20 $$ and the selling price is reduced by $$ \text{₹}2 $$ then the profit would be 27.5%. What is the original cost price (in $$ \text{₹} $$) of the article?

Two taps can fill a cistern in 4 hours and 20 hours, respectively. A third tap can empty it in 20 hours. How long (in hours) will it take to fill one-fourth of the empty cistern if all the taps are opened together?
Simplify: $$ 30 + \left[ \left\{ (-9) \times (26 - 11 + 3) \right\} \right] $$
The 7-digit number 12A916B is divisible by 24. What is the maximum value of (A+B)?
The sum of two positive numbers is 88 and their product is 1927. The positive difference between them is:
If a and b are two coprime numbers such that $$ a+b = 41 $$, then what is the maximum possible value of $$ a \times b $$?
A trader fixes the marked price of an article at $$ \text{₹}10,630 $$. He sells it to customers for $$ \text{₹}9244.1 $$ after giving certain discount. Find the discount percentage.

A solid cylinder of radius R and height H, is melted and recast into three identical cylinders each having the same height but original cylinder has half the radius of the original cylinder. What fraction of the original cylinder's volume remains unused?

Saumya has $$ \text{₹} 24,800 $$ in denominations of $$ \text{₹}500 $$ and $$ \text{₹}100 $$ notes. The number of $$ \text{₹}500 $$ notes is 4 more than the number of $$ \text{₹}100 $$ notes. Find the number of $$ \text{₹}100 $$ notes.

options.text: All bags have the same proportion.

questions.question_text: Three bags A, B, and C contain only red and blue marbles.In Bag A, the ratio of red to blue marbles is 3 : 5.In Bag B, the ratio of red to blue marbles is 4 : 1.In Bag C, the ratio of red to blue marbles is 2 : 3.Which bag has the highest proportion of red marbles?
A man sells a watch at a loss of 10%. Had he increased the selling price by $$ \text{₹}120 $$, the transaction would have resulted in a gain of 5%. Determine the selling price of the watch.
The simple interest on $$ \text{₹}55,000 $$ at 5.5% per annum simple rate of interest is $$ \text{₹} $$ ____ in 5.5 years.
A sum becomes $$ \text{₹}6,976 $$ after 2 years at 10% per annum compound interest, compounded annually. What was the principal amount (in $$ \text{₹} $$)?

From the top of a building 20 m high, the angles of depression of the top and the bottom of a tower standing on the same horizontal ground are observed to be $$ 30^\circ $$ and $$ 60^\circ $$, respectively. The height (in meter) of the tower is:(Round off your answer to two decimal places.)

A cyclist travels from point A to B and then returns from B to A along the same route. On the onward journey, he covers one-third of the distance at 15 km/hr and the remaining distance at 20 km/hr. On the return journey, he covers one-fourth of the distance at 10 km/hr and the remaining distance at 25 km/hr. Find the average for the entire trip. (Round off your answer to two decimal places.)

The following data represents the weekly wages (in $$ \text{₹} $$) of the employeesFind the arithmetic mean of the weekly wages (in $$ \text{₹} $$)Weekly wages (in $$ \text{₹} $$) | 900 | 1000 | 1100 | 1200 | 1300 | 1400 | 1500---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---Number of employees | 12 | 12 | 14 | 13 | 14 | 11 | 4
Eight years ago, the age of Mr Gupta was four times that of his nephew, Rohit. After 6 years, Mr Gupta's age will be twice that of Rohit. Find the present age of Mr Gupta.
What is the smallest natural number 'x' that needs to be subtracted from 13 and 25 so that the fourth proportional of the resulting numbers is 49?
Three positive numbers are in the ratio 3:5:7. If the sum of their squares is 2075, then the middle number is:

options.text: Delete all entered form data instantly
Moves cursor to subsequent input field or table cell
Prints the form with current entries
Saves document and closes the form view

Lok Adalat mainly resolves disputes through arbitration.
Lok Adalat decisions are not binding on parties.
Lok Adalat has the power to enforce its decisions like a civil court.

Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. You have to take the given Statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. You have to decide which conclusions logically follow/s from the given statements.Statements: I. All vans are trailers. No trailer is a scooter.Conclusion (I): No van is a scooter.Conclusion (II): Some scooters are trailers

In a certain code language,A – B means 'A is the wife of B'.A $$ \times $$ B means 'A is the brother of B'.A $$ \div $$ B means 'A is the father of B'.A & B means 'A is the daughter of B'.Based on the above, how is P related to U if P & D – M @ L $$ \times $$ Z?
If 3 is added to each odd digit and 2 is subtracted from each even digit in the number 3254316, then how many digits will appear more than once in the new number thus formed?
What should come in place of '?' in the given series?80 7 89 8 98 110 125 ?

Refer to the given letter series and answer the question that follows.(Left): S E S I B V V R R I V S S P B T K S A P P (Right)How many such consonants are there, each of which is immediately preceded by a vowel and also immediately followed by a vowel?

Refer to the following letter series and answer the question that follows. Counting to be done from left to right only.(Left):L R I D P E Q Y H D V Q U Y L X F H A U Z (Right)How many such vowels are there, each of which is immediately preceded by a consonant and also immediately followed by a consonant?

Which is the one that does not belong to that group?(Note: The odd man out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter cluster.)

You have to decide which conclusions logically follow/s from the given statements.Statements: I. Some pens are markers. All markers are stands.Conclusion (I): Some pens are stands.Conclusion (II): All stands are markers.

Rishi starts from Point A and drives 23 km towards the north. He then takes a left turn, drives 8 km, turns left and drives 23 km. He then takes a left turn and drives 5 km. He takes a final left turn, drives 5 km and stops at Point B. How far (horizontal distance) and towards which direction should he drive in order to reach Point A again? (All turns are 90-degree turns only, unless specified.)
Which of the following letter-number clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete?BVT 32, WOT 16, RLO 8, MGJ 4, ?
Each of the digits in the number 2748685 is arranged in ascending order from left to right. The position of how many digits will remain unchanged as compared to that in the original number?

Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to that group?(Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster pairs.)
In a certain code language, 'tale jaw bow' is coded as 'vl ud np' and 'law bow fresh' is coded as 'pq re ud'. How is 'bow' coded in the given language?
Select the pair which follows the same pattern as that followed by the two pairs given below. Both pairs follow the same pattern.VUT - PONTSU - PO

Each vowel in the word PREBLAST is changed to the letter immediately following it in the English alphabetical order and each consonant is changed to the letter immediately preceding it in the English alphabetical order. How many letters will appear exactly twice in the new group of letters thus formed?
Which two numbers should be interchanged to make the given equation correct?$$ (6 \times 14) + 2 + 18 \times 4 - 28 \div 7 + 34 = 87 $$

Seven people, S, T, I, C, K, E and R, are sitting in a row, facing north. Only two people sit between C and P. Only K sits to the right of E. Only one person sits between S and S. S sits at some place to the right of R but at some place to the left of T. Who sits third from the right end of the line?

Seven people, A, B, U, D, R, B and J, are sitting in a row, facing north.Only five people sit to the right of D. Only four people sit to the right of H. R is the immediate neighbour of A and D. J sits to the immediate right of B.How many people sit between U and D?

Seven boxes A, B, C, D, E, F and G are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only three boxes are kept between G and C. Only two boxes are kept between D and G. Only three boxes are kept between D and E. E is kept at some place above C. G is kept immediately above A. B is kept at some place above F. Box C or A is not kept immediately above or below D. Which box is kept immediately above F?
Which of the following letter-number clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete?ANS 3 BMR 6 KLO 9 MPS 12 ?

Six boxes D, E, F, G, H and A are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only three boxes are kept below A. Only two boxes are kept between A and E. No box is kept above H. H is kept at some place below F and at some place above D. How many boxes are kept between H and D?

In the following number-pairs, the second number is obtained by applying certain mathematical operations to the first number. Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as the numbers of the following sets.(NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into their constituent digits. E.g. 13 - Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)80, 104157, 172
In a certain code language, 'bat view month' is coded as 'nd wz nh' and 'oven month gum' is coded as 'on jg wz'. How is 'month' coded in the given language?

Seven boxes A, B, C, D, E, F and G are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only three boxes are kept below D. Only two boxes are kept between D and C. Box B is kept above G. B is kept at some place below A and some place above E. Which box is kept immediately above B?
